# UC-13.1.2 · Splunk Scheduled and Ad-hoc Search Concurrency Saturation, User-Quota Burn, and Search-Reaping Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch how many searches run at once versus the safe limits on the reporting computers, and we notice when work gets canceled or trimmed because time or space rules were hit. When traffic truly overwhelms the gates, we raise a clear signal so owners fix schedules, roles, or capacity before everyday reporting quietly falls behind.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk search-tier concurrency saturation by fusing metrics.log search_concurrency utilization against configured historical and realtime ceilings, scheduler.log skip and finalize narratives including reaper-adjacent quota exhaustion language, introspection PerProcess slot peaks with identity cardinality, and tstats scheduler volume sanity, enriched with pool inventory hints for role quota and disk or time window enforcement.

## Value

Platform leaders see mean time to innocence when dashboards feel sluggish or scheduled reports arrive late: this control quantifies whether the cluster is out of search slots, whether realtime panels are crowding historical work, whether user quotas and time windows are terminating searches, and whether finalize patterns imply systematic reclaim—before teams buy hardware or blame the wrong application tier.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_2_search_concurrency_saturation every fifteen minutes from the Monitoring Console or a fleet search head with earliest=-4h@h, keep splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv current for ownership and quota hints, tune hist and rt utilization thresholds per environment class, route critical rows to platform on-call with MC drilldowns, and archive weekly alert exports to your governance index.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_1_2_search_concurrency_saturation on a fifteen-minute schedule; Monitoring Console workload drilldowns; weekly CSV exports of alert results to a restricted platform_evidence index; splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv version hash archived with change tickets.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab search head, under change control, run a controlled burst of historical and realtime searches that drives active_hist_searches toward max_searches while scheduler.log emits skipped reasons mentioning concurrency or quota language; confirm platform_uc_13_1_2_search_concurrency_saturation returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within thirty minutes.

### Negative scenario

After the burst ends and metrics.log shows sustained low utilization with zero reaper_hits for four consecutive fifteen-minute bins, confirm severities return to low or medium and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with search-tier site reliability engineers, identity stewards who curate authorize.conf role quotas, and the observability team that operates the Monitoring Console plus fleet forwarding of index=_internal, index=_introspection, and index=_audit. This use case deliberately widens the lens beyond scheduled skip counting alone: it fuses metrics.log search_concurrency ceilings against live active historical and real-time search tallies, scheduler.log language that includes finalize and reaper-adjacent outcomes tied to quota exhaustion, introspection PerProcess rows that expose per-search provenance and slot pressure, and a tstats sanity lane on scheduler volume so a silent sourcetype outage cannot masquerade as a quiet cluster. UC-13.1.5 remains the deep scheduled-search starvation narrative with skip-rate denominators, savedsearch inventory joins, and cron herd economics. This card answers whether the platform is approaching hard utilization of historical and realtime slots across ad hoc, scheduled, and dashboard-driven workloads, whether user-level disk and time windows are mechanically terminating work, and whether dispatch finalization patterns imply the reaper path is reclaiming searches that lost their quota to complete.

Ground every deployment in limits.conf interaction with hardware. The scheduler stanza expresses how much historical scheduled work the node believes it can run relative to CPUs through keys such as max_searches_per_cpu, base_max_searches, auto_summary_perc, and max_searches_perc that reserve headroom for summarization and other protected classes. Real-time concurrency multipliers such as max_rt_search_multiplier scale how many simultaneous realtime searches may contend with historical runners on the same silicon. These knobs are not abstract trivia: they are the arithmetic behind metrics.log lines in group=search_concurrency where active_hist_searches approaches max_searches and active_rt_searches approaches max_rt_searches. When virtualization oversubscribes vCPU or when maintenance halves available cores without a matching limits review, utilization percentages climb while configuration files look unchanged.

authorize.conf translates identity into economics. Role stanzas bind srchJobsQuota, rtSrchJobsQuota, srchDiskQuota, and srchTimeWin to effective users. A mistaken mapping that places dozens of automation accounts into a powerful role can burn the shared pool even when each individual saved search looks innocent. Disk quota and time window enforcement surfaces in scheduler reasons and in audit narratives when searches finalize early or never expand their buckets as authors expected. Capacity planners should keep a curated splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv that lists each search head or pool label, environment tier, owner_team, documented srch_jobs_quota_hint and srch_disk_quota_hint from architecture reviews, and role_baseline_note pointers to the authoritative identity provider group mapping ticket.

Search head clustering changes where operators look for truth without changing the physics. Each member still emits local metrics.log and local scheduler.log streams; captain movement shifts who runs certain artifacts, yet concurrency remains member-local for many measurements. Job server routing discussions belong in cluster architecture reviews: understand which members accept interactive fan-out, how artifact replication pressure adds CPU drag, and why a deployer bundle push can transiently depress effective throughput. Pair this UC with UC-13.1.10 when member health, artifact replication, or captain instability could explain concurrency pain that limits.conf alone cannot fix.

Risk briefing: legitimate disaster tests and executive drill weekends may intentionally drive concurrency to the red zone under change control; require change_ticket_id correlation in sidecar indexes before paging product owners. Splunk Cloud estates may adjust effective pools through provider-side controls that do not appear in local on-disk files; keep provider correspondence in the same evidence pack as internal screenshots. DST transitions, leap seconds, and hypervisor clock corrections can skew fifteen-minute bins; compare wall-clock on the search head with NTP strata when anomalies cluster at time boundaries. Blue-green cutovers that migrate searches between pools can create a brief double-run illusion in forwarded logs if both pools index into the same destination; dedupe by search_head labels tied to CMDB.

Differentiation recap: slot utilization, scheduler finalize and reaper language, introspection provenance, and fleet-relative median context—not indexer queue fullness in isolation, not license pool dollar analytics, not forwarder phone-home connectivity, and not a pure scheduled-skip leaderboard without concurrency caps.

RBAC posture: grant authors read access to _internal and _introspection on search heads, read access to _audit per investigation policy, and read access to the lookup knowledge objects backing splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv. Restrict raw exports that contain usernames and search identities to platform teams; legal review should confirm retention aligns with workforce privacy rules.

Operational storytelling for executives should translate slot pressure into customer-visible risk: missed security correlation windows, stale executive KPI tiles, and delayed compliance attestations are downstream symptoms when concurrency pins for hours. Finance partners care because the first reflex is often to buy more search heads; this UC helps prove whether the immediate fix is hardware, schedule hygiene, role fairness, or a mis-sized realtime dashboard culture. Architecture review boards should treat recurring high hist_util_pct as a signal to revisit workload management pool definitions, per-role fair share, and ad hoc versus scheduled isolation policies rather than endlessly raising silent thresholds.

Field normalization discipline matters because metrics.log vocabulary evolved across minors. Some builds expose searches_started where others rely on active_hist_searches alone. Some expose base_max_searches in the same line as max_searches; others separate historical versus summarization budgets. Your coalesce ladders must stay current with release notes after upgrades, otherwise a benign field rename looks like a mysterious capacity miracle. Keep a version matrix in the runbook that lists which fields your estate confirmed on each search head class.

Index routing and storage economics interact with this signal. Forwarding _internal from dozens of members into a shared analytics cluster multiplies ingest license cost; finance may ask whether summarized arms are acceptable. The answer belongs in architecture policy: raw retention for fourteen days with hourly summary indexes for alert execution is a common compromise. Document the compromise so auditors know evidence still exists even when raw volume rolls to frozen paths.

Privacy and workforce monitoring cautions: usernames in scheduler and introspection streams can reveal who ran sensitive searches during incidents. Restrict dashboards, mask display names in executive views where policy requires, and route full-fidelity rows only to privileged responder roles. Pair with human resources guidance when investigations pivot from capacity to behavior.

Synthetic drill cadence: quarterly, execute a table-top where operators must explain hist_util_pct, rt_util_pct, and reaper_hits without opening the runbook. Gaps in fluency mean the UC will page into confusion during real outages.

Collector hardening: use dedicated forwarder identities for _internal shipping, rotate tokens, and monitor forwarder connectivity independently so a dead forwarder does not imply a healthy search tier.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes that multisearch fuses at query time, plus the inventory lookup that join enriches for ownership and quota hints.

Lane A, metrics.log search_concurrency: confirm index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd includes group=search_concurrency on every production search head and search head cluster member. Validate fields such as active_hist_searches, active_rt_searches, max_searches, max_rt_searches, and version-specific siblings your build exposes including max_searches_perc and auto_summary_perc when metrics.log prints them. Sampling should be frequent enough that fifteen-minute bins capture true peaks during top-of-hour report stampedes. If heavy filtering drops group=search_concurrency at the forwarder, this UC goes blind while users still complain.

Lane B, scheduler.log on sourcetype=scheduler: forward status=skipped lines with reasons, and preserve raw lines that mention dispatch_search_finalize, TERM_REASON, quota_exhausted, srchDiskQuota, srchTimeWin, and reaper-adjacent verbs your minor version emits. Keep TRUNCATE high enough that long reason strings survive for analytics. TIME_PREFIX must reflect splunkd scheduler timestamps so fifteen-minute bins align with metrics.log arms. Treat captain-only versus member-only visibility as a design choice: many estates forward all members to avoid blind spots after failover.

Lane C, introspection splunk_resource_usage with component=PerProcess: enable introspection engines on search heads per vendor guidance. Confirm search_props.app, search_props.user, search_props.search_id, search_props.role, and search_props.provenance populate during interactive and scheduled workloads. Disabling introspection to save a few gigabytes removes the arm that proves which identities occupy slots during saturation.

Lane D, tstats on scheduler volume: ensure _internal buckets remain searchable by tstats for sourcetype=scheduler in your version class. When security teams restrict tstats to summary metadata only, substitute a scheduled summary that mirrors tsch_rows counts per search_head and span, and document the substitution in the runbook.

Lookup maintenance: publish splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv with columns search_head (or sh_label alias), owner_team, env_tier, srch_jobs_quota_hint, srch_disk_quota_hint, and role_baseline_note. Refresh weekly or automate KV generation from CMDB. Stale ownership misroutes pages, it does not create false saturation.

Monitoring Console posture: validate that distributed health views show concurrent searches aligned with raw metrics.log peaks during a controlled load test. Workload management policies belong in operational reviews when concurrency chronically pins above policy even after schedule staggering.

Example CLI helpers for design reviews:

splunk btool limits list scheduler --debug
splunk btool authorize list YOUR_ROLE --debug
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=search_concurrency earliest=-1h | stats max(active_hist_searches) max(max_searches) by host
index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler TERM_REASON earliest=-1h | head 20

props.conf guidance: preserve LINE_BREAKER for metrics.log and scheduler.log; avoid aggressive TRUNCATE on scheduler reasons; map TIME_FORMAT consistently across clustered peers.

Volume and sampling notes: metrics.log can be chatty on busy search heads. Work with index administrators to confirm hot bucket sizing and maxDataSize for the _internal destination index still meets search performance targets when alert multisearch runs every fifteen minutes. When centralized logging introduces cross-site latency, widen earliest slightly in clones used for investigations so operators see the full ramp-up curve.

Security information and event management correlation: many enterprises route duplicate copies of scheduler excerpts to a security analytics cluster. Keep field names aligned between copies so this UC can be replayed in either environment during joint incidents without rewriting SPL.

Kubernetes and container notes: when search heads run as pods, node-level CPU limits from cgroup throttling can depress effective concurrency below what limits.conf implies from nominal CPU counts. Capture Kubernetes resource requests and limits in the inventory lookup as optional columns so responders see cgroup ceilings alongside Splunk configuration.

Edge cases for hybrid search: federated search and hybrid search extensions can shift where work executes. Document in the runbook when concurrent searches on member A include remote execution that does not reflect local metrics; otherwise investigators misattribute saturation.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_2_search_concurrency_saturation with a fifteen-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate search_head rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include recommended_action, fleet_hist_util_med, and reaper_hits in pager narratives so incident commanders open limits and authorize reviews before blaming applications at random.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans four arms—concurrency_metrics for cap math, scheduler_reaper for finalize and skip language, introspection_proc for PerProcess peak slots and identity cardinality, and tstats_scheduler for volume sanity—so a single silent lane cannot hide a real saturation. coalesce() ladders absorb field naming drift across Splunk minors for active versus max counters. streamstats retains short memory of utilization slopes without expensive transaction commands. eventstats computes fleet_hist_util_med per time bin so a lonely host above ninety percent is visible against peers. case() maps reaper density, quota language in reason blobs, historical and realtime utilization, and user fanout into critical, high, medium, and low tiers. join enriches ownership and quota hints from splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv using an inputlookup subsearch.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.2 concurrency saturation fuse: metrics.log search_concurrency caps, scheduler skip-reaper finalize, introspection PerProcess slots, tstats scheduler volume. Tunables earliest=-4h@h latest=now hist_warn=0.82 hist_page=0.94 rt_warn=0.78")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=search_concurrency earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval hist_active=tonumber(coalesce(active_hist_searches, searches_started, 0))
      | eval hist_limit=tonumber(coalesce(max_searches, max_hist_scheduled, base_max_searches, 0))
      | eval rt_active=tonumber(coalesce(active_rt_searches, 0))
      | eval rt_limit=tonumber(coalesce(max_rt_searches, 0))
      | eval rt_mult_obs=tonumber(coalesce(max_rt_search_multiplier, max_rt_multiplier, 1))
      | eval max_searches_perc_obs=tonumber(coalesce(max_searches_perc, auto_summary_perc, 0))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats max(hist_active) AS peak_hist_active max(hist_limit) AS cfg_hist_max max(rt_active) AS peak_rt_active max(rt_limit) AS cfg_rt_max max(rt_mult_obs) AS rt_mult_obs max(max_searches_perc_obs) AS max_searches_perc_obs BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="concurrency_metrics" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now (status=skipped OR match(_raw, "(?i)dispatch_search_finalize|TERM_REASON|quota_exhausted|reap|reaper|srchDiskQuota|srchTimeWin"))
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "", ""))))
      | eval provenance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(provenance, provenance_id, "scheduled"))))
      | eval disp_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status, "", ""))))
      | eval search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_id, savedsearch_id, ""))))
      | eval dispatch_etime=tonumber(coalesce(dispatch_time, dispatch_etime, 0))
      | eval runtime=tonumber(coalesce(run_time, runtime, 0))
      | eval role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(role, "", ""))))
      | eval reaper_signal=if(match(_raw, "(?i)dispatch_search_finalize|TERM_REASON|reap|reaper|quota_exhausted|skipped"), 1, 0)
      | eval sched_reason=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(reason, message, ""))), 1, 260)
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS sched_events sum(reaper_signal) AS reaper_hits dc(user_id) AS distinct_users values(sched_reason) AS sched_reason_mv dc(search_id) AS search_id_fanout BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="scheduler_reaper" ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now component=PerProcess
      | where isnotnull('search_props.app') OR isnotnull('search_props.search')
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.user', user, "", ""))))
      | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.app', app, "", ""))))
      | eval search_id=trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.search_id', search_id, "")))
      | eval provenance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.provenance', provenance, "process"))))
      | eval role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.role', role, "", ""))))
      | eval runtime=tonumber(coalesce(run_time, runtime, 0))
      | eval pid=tonumber(coalesce(pid, process_id, 0))
      | eval node_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node_type, "search_head"))))
      | eval conc_slot=tonumber(coalesce(active_hist_searches, searches_started, 0))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats max(conc_slot) AS intro_peak_hist dc(search_id) AS intro_search_ids max(runtime) AS intro_runtime_max max(pid) AS intro_pid_max BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="introspection_proc" ]
    [ | tstats count AS tsch_rows WHERE index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=15m splunk_server
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="tstats_scheduler"
      | fields _time search_head tsch_rows lane ]
| fillnull value=0 peak_hist_active cfg_hist_max peak_rt_active cfg_rt_max sched_events reaper_hits distinct_users intro_peak_hist intro_search_ids tsch_rows
| stats max(_time) AS last_evt max(peak_hist_active) AS peak_hist_active max(cfg_hist_max) AS cfg_hist_max max(peak_rt_active) AS peak_rt_active max(cfg_rt_max) AS cfg_rt_max max(rt_mult_obs) AS rt_mult_obs max(max_searches_perc_obs) AS max_searches_perc_obs max(sched_events) AS sched_events max(reaper_hits) AS reaper_hits max(distinct_users) AS distinct_users max(intro_peak_hist) AS intro_peak_hist max(intro_search_ids) AS intro_search_ids max(intro_runtime_max) AS intro_runtime_max max(intro_pid_max) AS intro_pid_max max(tsch_rows) AS tsch_rows values(lane) AS lane_vals values(sched_reason_mv) AS sched_reason_mv BY _time search_head
| eval hist_util_pct=if(cfg_hist_max>0, round(100*peak_hist_active/cfg_hist_max,2), null())
| eval rt_util_pct=if(cfg_rt_max>0, round(100*peak_rt_active/cfg_rt_max,2), null())
| sort 0 search_head - _time
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f last(hist_util_pct) AS hist_util_lag1 last(rt_util_pct) AS rt_util_lag1 BY search_head
| eventstats median(hist_util_pct) AS fleet_hist_util_med BY _time
| eval reason_blob=coalesce(mvjoin(sched_reason_mv, " | "), "")
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(reaper_hits,0)>=4 OR match(lower(reason_blob), "quota_exhausted|srchdiskquota|srchtimewin"), "critical",
    coalesce(hist_util_pct,0)>=94 OR coalesce(rt_util_pct,0)>=92, "critical",
    coalesce(hist_util_pct,0)>=82 OR coalesce(rt_util_pct,0)>=78 OR coalesce(reaper_hits,0)>=1, "high",
    coalesce(sched_events,0)>=250 AND coalesce(distinct_users,0)>=20, "medium",
    coalesce(intro_peak_hist,0)>=coalesce(cfg_hist_max,999999)*0.80 AND cfg_hist_max>0, "high",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND coalesce(reaper_hits,0)>=1, "inspect_scheduler_log_reaper_finalize_lines_review_limits_authorize_quotas",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND coalesce(hist_util_pct,0)>=82, "raise_capacity_or_stagger_workload_review_max_searches_per_cpu_auto_summary_perc",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND coalesce(rt_util_pct,0)>=78, "reduce_realtime_dashboard_load_review_max_rt_search_multiplier",
    severity="medium", "trace_user_fanout_tighten_role_srchjobsquota_and_disk_time_windows",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_refresh_pool_inventory_lookup")
| join type=left max=0 search_head
    [| inputlookup splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv
     | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_head, sh_label, ""))))
     | fields search_head owner_team env_tier srch_jobs_quota_hint srch_disk_quota_hint role_baseline_note ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned")
| eval env_tier=coalesce(env_tier, "unknown")
| table _time search_head hist_util_pct rt_util_pct peak_hist_active cfg_hist_max peak_rt_active cfg_rt_max reaper_hits sched_events distinct_users intro_peak_hist tsch_rows fleet_hist_util_med severity recommended_action owner_team env_tier last_evt
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[platform_uc_13_1_2_search_concurrency_saturation_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_2_search_concurrency_saturation | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance notes: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on _time and search_head, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap subsearch fan-out on tiny search heads used only for alert execution.



### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the search head under study. Open metrics.log and confirm group=search_concurrency lines arrive with non-null active and max fields during interactive searching. Compare peak arithmetic by hand for one interval against hist_util_pct in Splunk.

Replay scheduler.log around the same window and verify that skipped reasons and any TERM_REASON or finalize language you expect appear in index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler with matching _time ordering. When raw files show reaper signals but Splunk shows zero reaper_hits, fix LINE_BREAKER or sourcetype routing before trusting the alert.

Use Monitoring Console workload panels as a second opinion: concurrent search visuals should rise and fall with peak_hist_active within one bin. Divergence implies the monitoring search head cannot read a partitioned member or that RBAC blocks _internal.

Inspect btool effective limits for [scheduler] and compare max_searches_per_cpu against physical and effective CPUs. Reconcile authorize.conf quotas for the noisiest users surfaced in distinct_users spikes.

Deliberate load test: in a non-production pool, run a controlled burst of historical and realtime searches under vendor guidance, confirm critical or high severity appears with coherent recommended_action, then stop the load and confirm decay within fifteen minutes.

Negative test: on a calm cluster after quiet hours, confirm critical and high severities stay empty across four consecutive runs.

Job Inspector: confirm scanned event counts remain proportional to _internal volume; unexpected explosion suggests unbounded macros or accidental all-time wildcards.

Cross-check with REST when appropriate: the services server introspection endpoints can expose concurrent search counts that should move in the same direction as metrics.log during controlled tests. Disagreement indicates parsing or forwarding gaps rather than mystical capacity.

Documentation hygiene: store screenshots of Monitoring Console workload pages next to Splunk alert rows in the ticketing system so future reviewers inherit visual context without rerunning searches.

Retention alignment: if _internal retention is shorter than the investigation window you promise auditors, extend retention or export summaries to an evidence index so historical replay remains honest.

Training developers: application teams often misunderstand realtime versus historical modes. Offer a short internal wiki page that explains why twelve realtime panels per user can crowd a shared cluster even when each panel looks small alone.

Governance linkage: map recurring critical severity to quarterly architecture forum agendas so leadership funds the right remediation lane instead of one-off firefighting.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Historical slot saturation with polite skip reasons: stagger cron schedules, raise max_searches_per_cpu only after CPU headroom proof, and review auto_summary_perc so summarization keeps protected budget without starving interactive work.

Case 2 — Realtime dashboards pinning max_rt_searches: replace abusive all-time realtime panels, convert to historical with reasonable earliest bounds, and review max_rt_search_multiplier against vendor guidance for the hardware class.

Case 3 — srchDiskQuota and srchTimeWin churn visible in reason text: identify runaway searches with huge result sets, tighten role quotas for sandbox accounts, and coach authors on stats-first patterns.

Case 4 — Search head cluster captain flapping depressing usable capacity: restore UC-13.1.10 health before chasing limits; avoid duplicating captain remediation steps inside this saved search narrative.

Case 5 — Deployer bundle push transiently elevates introspection concurrency: annotate maintenance windows and suppress pages when change metadata proves bundle replication is the forcing function.

Case 6 — Identity provider group change ballooning srchJobsQuota: revert mapping under change control, replay audit trails for privilege adds, and update role_baseline_note in the inventory lookup.

Case 7 — Virtualization oversubscription lowering effective CPU: treat limits as suspect until hypervisor ratios are honest; prefer adding vCPU or moving pools over endless threshold tuning.

Case 8 — tstats arm flat while search finds scheduler events interactively: repair tsidx or index definitions for _internal on the search peer executing the alert; do not mute the UC without fixing root cause.

Case 9 — Forwarder duplication double-counting metrics on a merged host label: dedupe at ingest or tighten host naming so search_head keys remain one physical member per row.

Case 10 — Provider-side Splunk Cloud pool throttle: open support with tsch_rows and hist_util_pct timelines; internal limits.conf may look generous while effective pools are not.

Case 11 — Penetration test red-team search storms: correlate distinct_users spikes with security calendars; downgrade severity only when ticketed and time-bounded.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists concurrency metrics, scheduler reaper, introspection PerProcess, and tstats arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, tstats, join, and inputlookup appear; closing table lists sixteen analyst-facing columns including fleet median and ownership context.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.2 concurrency saturation fuse: metrics.log search_concurrency caps, scheduler skip-reaper finalize, introspection PerProcess slots, tstats scheduler volume. Tunables earliest=-4h@h latest=now hist_warn=0.82 hist_page=0.94 rt_warn=0.78")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=search_concurrency earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval hist_active=tonumber(coalesce(active_hist_searches, searches_started, 0))
      | eval hist_limit=tonumber(coalesce(max_searches, max_hist_scheduled, base_max_searches, 0))
      | eval rt_active=tonumber(coalesce(active_rt_searches, 0))
      | eval rt_limit=tonumber(coalesce(max_rt_searches, 0))
      | eval rt_mult_obs=tonumber(coalesce(max_rt_search_multiplier, max_rt_multiplier, 1))
      | eval max_searches_perc_obs=tonumber(coalesce(max_searches_perc, auto_summary_perc, 0))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats max(hist_active) AS peak_hist_active max(hist_limit) AS cfg_hist_max max(rt_active) AS peak_rt_active max(rt_limit) AS cfg_rt_max max(rt_mult_obs) AS rt_mult_obs max(max_searches_perc_obs) AS max_searches_perc_obs BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="concurrency_metrics" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now (status=skipped OR match(_raw, "(?i)dispatch_search_finalize|TERM_REASON|quota_exhausted|reap|reaper|srchDiskQuota|srchTimeWin"))
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "", ""))))
      | eval provenance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(provenance, provenance_id, "scheduled"))))
      | eval disp_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status, "", ""))))
      | eval search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_id, savedsearch_id, ""))))
      | eval dispatch_etime=tonumber(coalesce(dispatch_time, dispatch_etime, 0))
      | eval runtime=tonumber(coalesce(run_time, runtime, 0))
      | eval role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(role, "", ""))))
      | eval reaper_signal=if(match(_raw, "(?i)dispatch_search_finalize|TERM_REASON|reap|reaper|quota_exhausted|skipped"), 1, 0)
      | eval sched_reason=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(reason, message, ""))), 1, 260)
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS sched_events sum(reaper_signal) AS reaper_hits dc(user_id) AS distinct_users values(sched_reason) AS sched_reason_mv dc(search_id) AS search_id_fanout BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="scheduler_reaper" ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now component=PerProcess
      | where isnotnull('search_props.app') OR isnotnull('search_props.search')
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.user', user, "", ""))))
      | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.app', app, "", ""))))
      | eval search_id=trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.search_id', search_id, "")))
      | eval provenance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.provenance', provenance, "process"))))
      | eval role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.role', role, "", ""))))
      | eval runtime=tonumber(coalesce(run_time, runtime, 0))
      | eval pid=tonumber(coalesce(pid, process_id, 0))
      | eval node_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node_type, "search_head"))))
      | eval conc_slot=tonumber(coalesce(active_hist_searches, searches_started, 0))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats max(conc_slot) AS intro_peak_hist dc(search_id) AS intro_search_ids max(runtime) AS intro_runtime_max max(pid) AS intro_pid_max BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="introspection_proc" ]
    [ | tstats count AS tsch_rows WHERE index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=15m splunk_server
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="tstats_scheduler"
      | fields _time search_head tsch_rows lane ]
| fillnull value=0 peak_hist_active cfg_hist_max peak_rt_active cfg_rt_max sched_events reaper_hits distinct_users intro_peak_hist intro_search_ids tsch_rows
| stats max(_time) AS last_evt max(peak_hist_active) AS peak_hist_active max(cfg_hist_max) AS cfg_hist_max max(peak_rt_active) AS peak_rt_active max(cfg_rt_max) AS cfg_rt_max max(rt_mult_obs) AS rt_mult_obs max(max_searches_perc_obs) AS max_searches_perc_obs max(sched_events) AS sched_events max(reaper_hits) AS reaper_hits max(distinct_users) AS distinct_users max(intro_peak_hist) AS intro_peak_hist max(intro_search_ids) AS intro_search_ids max(intro_runtime_max) AS intro_runtime_max max(intro_pid_max) AS intro_pid_max max(tsch_rows) AS tsch_rows values(lane) AS lane_vals values(sched_reason_mv) AS sched_reason_mv BY _time search_head
| eval hist_util_pct=if(cfg_hist_max>0, round(100*peak_hist_active/cfg_hist_max,2), null())
| eval rt_util_pct=if(cfg_rt_max>0, round(100*peak_rt_active/cfg_rt_max,2), null())
| sort 0 search_head - _time
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f last(hist_util_pct) AS hist_util_lag1 last(rt_util_pct) AS rt_util_lag1 BY search_head
| eventstats median(hist_util_pct) AS fleet_hist_util_med BY _time
| eval reason_blob=coalesce(mvjoin(sched_reason_mv, " | "), "")
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(reaper_hits,0)>=4 OR match(lower(reason_blob), "quota_exhausted|srchdiskquota|srchtimewin"), "critical",
    coalesce(hist_util_pct,0)>=94 OR coalesce(rt_util_pct,0)>=92, "critical",
    coalesce(hist_util_pct,0)>=82 OR coalesce(rt_util_pct,0)>=78 OR coalesce(reaper_hits,0)>=1, "high",
    coalesce(sched_events,0)>=250 AND coalesce(distinct_users,0)>=20, "medium",
    coalesce(intro_peak_hist,0)>=coalesce(cfg_hist_max,999999)*0.80 AND cfg_hist_max>0, "high",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND coalesce(reaper_hits,0)>=1, "inspect_scheduler_log_reaper_finalize_lines_review_limits_authorize_quotas",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND coalesce(hist_util_pct,0)>=82, "raise_capacity_or_stagger_workload_review_max_searches_per_cpu_auto_summary_perc",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND coalesce(rt_util_pct,0)>=78, "reduce_realtime_dashboard_load_review_max_rt_search_multiplier",
    severity="medium", "trace_user_fanout_tighten_role_srchjobsquota_and_disk_time_windows",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_refresh_pool_inventory_lookup")
| join type=left max=0 search_head
    [| inputlookup splunk_search_head_pool_inventory.csv
     | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_head, sh_label, ""))))
     | fields search_head owner_team env_tier srch_jobs_quota_hint srch_disk_quota_hint role_baseline_note ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned")
| eval env_tier=coalesce(env_tier, "unknown")
| table _time search_head hist_util_pct rt_util_pct peak_hist_active cfg_hist_max peak_rt_active cfg_rt_max reaper_hits sched_events distinct_users intro_peak_hist tsch_rows fleet_hist_util_med severity recommended_action owner_team env_tier last_evt
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=false latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS search_head
| where cpu_pct>90 OR mem_used>92
| table search_head cpu_pct mem_used
```

## Visualization

Fifteen-minute stepped area charts for hist_util_pct and rt_util_pct per search_head, single-value tiles for reaper_hits and fleet_hist_util_med, severity-colored table with drilldowns to metrics.log slices, scheduler reasons, introspection rows, and inventory metadata.

## Known False Positives

Planned load and disaster exercises can pin concurrency without malfunction when change tickets document the window; require ticket correlation before executive escalation. Captain maintenance and bundle replication often create short peaks in PerProcess counts that self-resolve; pair with UC-13.1.10 timelines rather than permanent limit raises. Penetration tests and purple-team search storms inflate distinct_users and sched_events while the platform is behaving correctly; downgrade only with security calendar proof. Splunk Cloud pool adjustments may change effective throughput without local file edits; provider emails belong in the evidence pack to explain benign shifts. Metrics duplication from misconfigured forwarding can exaggerate peaks until host keys are deduplicated. Rare parser truncations can hide reaper tokens while raw files still show them; fix TRUNCATE instead of muting the alert. DST boundaries can split bins oddly; compare wall-clock histograms when incidents cluster at exactly the clock shift hour.
